results.title: Saikat Bhattacharya
highlights:
Data Scientist staff at [Lam Research](https://www.linkedin.com/company/lam-research)
...
I am a Staff Data Scientist with 12+ years of experience specializing in Natural Language Processing (NLP), Generative AI, and large-scale machine learning systems. My work bridges deep technical expertise with business impact—driving innovation, shaping AI strategy, and delivering scalable solutions that create measurable value. Over the years, I have led end-to-end AI initiatives, from research and prototyping to production-grade deployments across LLMs, RAG pipelines, semantic search, conversational AI, and text generation systems. I bring strong expertise in fine-tuning large models, developing evaluation frameworks for LLM consistency and reliability, and optimizing inference pipelines for real-world enterprise use cases. Beyond technical delivery, I am passionate about leadership and team development. I actively mentor data scientists and ML engineers, fostering growth and advancing best practices in model governance, ethical AI, and scalable machine learning. Partnering closely with product, engineering, and business leaders, I ensure that AI solutions are not just cutting-edge, but also strategically aligned with organizational goals. Key highlights include: • Spearheaded enterprise GenAI adoption programs, accelerating decision-making and reducing operational costs. • Developed and deployed AI-driven automation systems impacting global teams and thousands of end-users. • Designed evaluation frameworks for LLM consistency, improving trust and stability in RAG systems. My next career goal is to transition into a Data Science Manager role, where I can combine technical leadership with organizational strategy—building high-performing teams and shaping the future of AI adoption.
...

...
I’m a data scientist with over four years of hands-on experience applying machine learning and Python to develop impactful, data-driven solutions across multiple industries. I specialize in turning complex data into actionable insights that drive operational efficiency, strategic decision-making, and enhanced user experiences. My work spans the full data science lifecycle—from data preparation and feature engineering to model development, deployment, and optimization. With a strong foundation in analytics and data engineering, I’m skilled at building robust, scalable solutions that solve real business problems. I hold a Bachelor of Engineering degree, with a strong academic focus on mathematics, statistics, and core engineering principles—skills that continue to shape my approach to problem-solving in fast-paced, data-centric environments. I’m passionate about using data to create measurable impact and thrive in collaborative settings where cross-functional teams come together to align technology with business goal.

...
Lam Research is intensifying its focus on AI-enabled semiconductor manufacturing as rising global demand for AI chips continues reshaping the chipmaking ecosystem. The company is integrating advanced sensing and AI capabilities into its semiconductor manufacturing tools to improve productivity, reduce defects, and optimize wafer yields.
...
CEO 𝗧𝗶𝗺 𝗔𝗿𝗰𝗵𝗲𝗿 stated that Lam’s strategic focus over the next two years is to deploy more sensors across its equipment, generating real-time data that AI systems can analyze to identify inefficiencies and predict manufacturing issues earlier in the production cycle. Archer noted, “The more data you collect from the machine itself, or from the wafer, the better your models can be about predicting what’s happening.” He further emphasized that AI is helping identify system conditions “that we didn’t know were a problem before.”
...
Alongside AI integration, Lam Research is expanding its U.S. footprint with additional investments planned in Arizona and California to support major semiconductor customers including TSMC and Micron. Lam Research is dedicated to excellence in the design and engineering of etch and deposition products for the semiconductor industry. results.title: Staff AI Engineer - Design Automation Job in San Jose | Sandisk
highlights:
Staff AI Engineer - Design Automation Job in San Jose | Sandisk
...
Machine-Learning Alert
...
# Staff AI Engineer - Design Automation
...
Sandisk Drive 991, San Jose
...
- We require a masters degree in Electrical Engineering, Computer Engineering, Computer Science, or a closely related discipline. - We look for at least 3 years of industry experience. - We need familiarity with semiconductors or EDA, such as RTL design, verification, physical design, or EDA tool development. - We expect a foundational understanding of AI/ML, including how models are trained and evaluated. - We need strong programming ability and a track record of building reliable, production-ready systems. - We value systems thinking and the ability to diagnose subtle, complex technical issues. - We require clear communication skills and the ability to connect different technical disciplines. - A PhD in EE, CE, CS, or a related field is preferred. - Deep expertise in semiconductor areas such as verification methodologies, synthesis, timing closure, or DFT is preferred. - Deep expertise in AI/ML, including model architectures, NLP, or reinforcement learning, is preferred. - Hands-on experience with LLMs, RAG architectures, or agentic systems is preferred. - Experience with EDA tool internals or automation scripting is preferred. - Publications or notable projects in semiconductor or AI/ML are preferred.
...
## Responsibilities
...
- We design and build AI agents that automate multi-step semiconductor workflows. - We develop the agent platform, including orchestration, tool integration, observability, and evaluation capabilities. - We work closely with design and verification engineers to understand pain points and uncover high-impact automation opportunities. - We create methods to ground agents in domain knowledge such as specifications, design rules, and verification intent. - We build rigorous evaluation frameworks to measure reliability, correctness, and safety. - We debug difficult issues across the full stack, from model behavior to infrastructure and domain-specific edge cases. - We ship systems that perform reliably in real production settings, not just impressive demonstrations.
...
We are Sandisk, a global innovator in Flash and advanced memory technologies, with a long history of shaping the digital world through performance, quality, and manufacturing excellence. We operate at the intersection of customer ambition and technological progress, and our facilities have been recognized by the World Economic Forum for advanced 4IR innovation and sustainability. In this role, we are building AI agents that automate complex semiconductor engineering workflows, and we offer a comprehensive compensation and benefits package that includes incentive plans, potential equity participation, paid time off, insurance coverage, retirement support, tuition reimbursement, transit benefits, and employee wellness programs. We are committed to equal opportunity, inclusion, and supporting applicants with disabilities.

# Machine Learning Engineer (NCG 2026)
...
拉股份有限公司 Ast
...
Astera Labs (NASDAQ: ALAB) provides rack-scale AI infrastructure through purpose-built connectivity solutions. By collaborating with hyperscalers and ecosystem partners, Astera Labs enables organizations to unlock the full potential of modern AI. Astera Labs’ Intelligent Connectivity Platform integrates CXL®, Ethernet, NVLink, PCIe®, and UALink™ semiconductor-based technologies with the company’s COSMOS software suite to unify diverse components into cohesive, flexible systems that deliver end-to-end scale-up, and scale-out connectivity. The company’s custom connectivity solutions business complements its standards-based portfolio, enabling customers to deploy tailored architectures to meet their unique infrastructure requirements. Discover more at www.asteralabs.com.

results.title: Senior Staff Engineer, AI Software  at Samsung Semiconductor · JobsRadar
highlights:
Engineer, AI Software at
...
Senior Staff Engineer
...
The AGI (Artificial General Intelligence) Computing Lab is dedicated to solving the complex system-level challenges posed by the growing demands of future AI/ML workloads. Our team is committed to designing and developing scalable platforms that can effectively handle the computational and memory requirements of these workloads while minimizing energy consumption and maximizing performance. To achieve this goal, we collaborate closely with both hardware and software engineers to identify and address the unique challenges posed by AI/ML workloads and to explore new computing abstractions that can provide a better balance between the hardware and software components of our systems. Additionally, we continuously conduct research and development in emerging technologies and trends across memory, computing, interconnect, and AI/ML, ensuring that our platforms are always equipped to handle the most demanding workloads of the future. By working together as a dedicated and passionate team, we aim to revolutionize the way AI/ML applications are deployed and executed, ultimately contributing to the advancement of AGI in an affordable and sustainable manner. Join us in our passion to shape the future of computing!

results.title: Machine Learning Operations Contractor | Careerport
highlights: a Machine Learning Operations (MLOps) Contractor to drive semiconductor laser design and manufacturing excellence through the development and deployment of AI/ML within production. The emphasis will be on yield improvement, screening accuracy, and design optimization. The MLOps Contractor will develop and validate models based on extensive production data and deploy scalable models through a combination of cloud-based infrastructure and on-premises high performance inference. This position represents a unique opportunity to make an impactful contribution to the laser manufacturing industry and to pioneer AI/ML integration across Coherent international manufacturing sites. This is a contract-based position, ideal for a data scientist or engineer with strong AI/ML expertise and a background in photonics, semiconductor device physics, or semiconductor manufacturing.   Primary Duties & Responsibilities Data Processing and Model Development Enhance data pipelines across wafer processes, die-level test, and experimentation. Develop supervised and unsupervised models for yield prediction and performance screening. The ideal candidate will be proficient in data pre-processing, feature extraction, dimensionality reduction, clustering, classification, and regression.  Experience with NN architectures such as CNNs, AEs, GANs and with ensemble learning methods including gradient boosting and random forests is preferred.   Validation and Deployment  Apply model selection to identify effective approaches and validate models using known techniques. Integrate domain knowledge from semiconductor laser physics into ML models for improved interpretability. Familiarity with inverse design concepts is a plus. Collaboration and Reporting Work closely with photonics researchers and process engineers to align ML approaches with experimental and production objectives and quantify cost-benefit analysis. Document methodologies, model performance, and research findings clearly. Provide regular updates and deliverables to project stakeholders. Education & Experience B.S. with 5-year industry experience or M.S./ Ph.D. with 2-year industry experience in Electrical Engineering, Computer Science, Physics, Photonics, or related field. Proven track record in AI/ML deployment and integration within high visibility projects.  Skills Expertise with ML frameworks: TensorFlow, PyTorch, scikit-learn. Proficiency in both Python and C/C++ programming data structures for performance optimization Experience in high-performance ML inference (LibTorch, CUDA, ONNX) Experience with cloud-based ML platforms (AWS, GCP, Azure) and MLOps platforms such as Kubernetes Ability to visualize and communicate insights effectively Background in photonics, semiconductor devices, or manufacturing yield optimization is a strong plus Working Conditions On-site 6-12 months temporary assignment Safety Requirements All employees are required to follow the site EHS procedures and Coherent Corp. Corporate EHS standards. Quality and Environmental Responsibilities Depending on location, this position may be responsible for the execution and maintenance of the ISO 9000, 9001, 14001 and/or other applicable standards that may apply to the relevant roles and responsibilities within the Quality Management System and Environmental Management System. Culture Commitment Ensure adherence to company’s values (ICARE) in all aspects of your position at Coherent Corp.: I ntegrity – Create an Environment of Trust C ollaboration – Innovate Through the Sharing of Ideas A ccountability – Own the Process and the Outcome R espect – Recognize the Value in Everyone E nthusiasm – Find a Sense of Purpose in Work Coherent Corp. is an equal opportunity/affirmative action employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to sex, gender identity, sexual orientation, race, color, religion, national origin, disability, protected Veteran status, age, or any other

results.title: LLM Engineer job with VIB | 12860950
highlights:
LLM Engineer job with VIB | 12860950
...
# LLM Engineer
...
VIB is a leading life sciences research institute in Flanders, Belgium, with a strong focus on translating basic scientific discoveries into benefits for society. With the launch of VIB.AI, we are expanding our mission to harness the power of artificial intelligence for life sciences research, innovation, and impact.
...
The VIB Center for Neuroscience Leuven (CNL) investigates the mechanisms of brain function and neurological disease across the scales of biology, spanning neurodegeneration, neurodevelopment, synaptic biology, and computational neuroscience. AI and large language models have transformative potential across these research areas.
...
We are now looking for an LLM Engineer in a shared position between VIB.AI's Machine Learning Expertise Unit and the Center for Neuroscience. You will split your time between building institute-wide AI tools and applying them directly to neuroscience research challenges.
...
As an LLM Engineer in this shared position, you will:
...
- Develop and deploy LLM-based pilot projects tailored to neuroscience research at the CNL, applying AI to questions in molecular, cellular and circuit neuroscience, neurodegeneration, neurodevelopment and disease mechanisms.
...
- Build and maintain components of VIB's institutional intelligence layer, including agentic bioinformatics workflows, retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) systems, and AI-assisted experimental design tools.
...
- Design, implement, and evaluate LLM-based solutions for scientific text mining, multimodal integration of literature and biological data, and knowledge extraction to support discovery.
...
- Collaborate with wet-lab and computational neuroscience researchers to identify opportunities where generative AI and LLMs can accelerate scientific progress.
...
- Work with the Machine Learning Expertise Unit and VIB's AI Studio to translate pilot projects into practical tools that serve the broader VIB community.
...
- Promote ethical, transparent, and responsible use of LLMs in line with best practices and international standards.
...
- Mine opportunities and data at the center’s advanced technology expertise centers and affiliated central core facilities.
...
- Leverage VIB's Data Core services, including high-performance computing pipelines and large-scale GPU resources, to scale LLM development and deployment.
...
- PhD in machine learning, computer science, computational biology, computational neuroscience, statistics, or a related field.
- Strong experience with large language models, including pretraining, fine-tuning, prompt engineering, and evaluation.
- Knowledge of modern deep learning frameworks (e.g., PyTorch, JAX, TensorFlow).
- Experience with real-world applications of LLMs in scientific or biomedical contexts is a plus.
...
- Familiarity with neuroscience data types (imaging, electrophysiology, genomics, proteomics, or clinical data) is a plus but not required.
...
- Ability to work collaboratively across disciplines and communicate effectively with researchers from different backgrounds.
- A strong interest in applying LLMs to impactful life science and neuroscience problems.
...
- The opportunity to pioneer applications of LLMs in neuroscience and the broader life sciences.
...
- A unique position at the interface of an AI platform team and a world-class neuroscience research center, with exposure to both engineering and scientific challenges.
...
- Direct access to VIB's Data Core services, which provide expert support in data management and high-performance computing, including optimized pipelines and large-scale GPU resources.
...
Please complete the application through the VIB online application tool. A complete application file should contain the following documents:
...
For further information, please send an email to stein.aerts@kuleuven.be or joost.schymkowitz@kuleuven.be with subject title Inquiry: LLM Engineer CNL/VIB.AI.

# Scientific Lead, Generative AI Engineer, Applied Intelligence for Discovery
...
Employer Eli Lilly and Company Location San Francisco, California Start date Jun 15, 2026
...
## Job Details
...
We are building something unprecedented, an AI foundation that will fundamentally change how drug discovery research is conducted.
...
The Applied Intelligence for Discovery (AI4D) team is a newly formed group within Lilly Research Laboratories that operates at the intersection of scientific delivery and core platform development. AI4D’s mission is to connecting scientists to petabyte-scale data through natural language interfaces, automated analysis workflows, and intelligent search — and to convert early deployments into repeatable system standards and evaluation practices that scale across therapeutic areas.
...
As a Generative AI Engineer, you will design, build, and operate the core AI systems that power this transformation: retrieval-augmented generation over internal scientific documents, text-to-SQL over complex omics databases, agentic workflows that automate multi-step analyses, and the evaluation infrastructure that able the next-generation of medicines for patients.
...
Key Responsibilities
...
Design, build, and optimize RAG pipelines over internal publications, study reports, electronic lab notebooks, and other scientific documents
...
Build hybrid retrieval systems combining vector search with structured metadata, knowledge graphs, and ontology-aware filtering
...
Build and optimize text-to-SQL systems over Lilly’s databases, enabling scientists to query gene expression, proteomics, pathway, and variant data through natural language
...
Develop schema documentation, semantic annotations, and gold-standard question/SQL pairs that bridge how scientists think about data and how it is stored
...
Implement multi-step reasoning approaches (chain-of-thought, self-correction, Reflexion loops) to improve accuracy on complex scientific queries
...
Design agentic AI workflows that chain database queries, bioinformatics tools, literature search, and visualization into automated multi-step scientific analyses
...
Evaluate and integrate emerging orchestration frameworks (LangGraph, CrewAI, custom architectures) for scientific use cases
...
Build evaluation frameworks measuring accuracy, reliability, and scientific validity of AI outputs
...
PhD in Computer Science, Data Science, or a related technical field with 0-3+ years of experience; or equivalent experience building production LLM systems; MS in Computer Science, Data Science, or a related technical field with 5+ years of experience; or equivalent experience building production LLM systems
...
Experience building LLM-powered applications, including at least two of: RAG systems, text-to-SQL, agentic workflows, or fine-tuning pipelines
...
Strong software engineering skills in Python with experience building production-grade systems
...
Deep familiarity with the modern LLM ecosystem: embedding models, vector databases, and orchestration frameworks
...
Experience designing evaluation frameworks for LLM systems — systematic approaches to measuring accuracy, detecting hallucinations, and tracking regressions
...
Comfort working with complex, heterogeneous data — databases with hundreds of tables, specialized schemas, or domain-specific vocabularies
...
Familiarity with biomedical data types (omics, clinical, molecular) or scientific databases
...
Experience with MLOps/LLMOps tooling: experiment tracking, model registries, prompt versioning, A/B testing for AI systems
...
Knowledge of biomedical ontologies (Gene Ontology, MeSH, ChEBI) or experience integrating domain-specific knowledge into LLM systems

Application deadline: August 31, 2026 About us VIB is a leading life sciences research institute in Flanders, Belgium, with a strong focus on translating basic scientific discoveries into benefits for society. With the launch of VIB.AI, we are expanding our mission to harness the power of artificial intelligence for life sciences research, innovation, and impact. We are now looking for a Machine Learning (ML) Engineer to join the Machine Learning Expertise Unit at VIB.AI. Your role As an ML Engineer, you will: Develop and deploy ML solutions in close collaboration with researchers across VIB centers, applying state-of-the-art methods to cutting-edge biomedical questions.Design, implement, and evaluate ML and foundation-model approaches, including LLM-based solutions, for scientific text mining, multimodal integration of literature and biological data, and knowledge extraction to support discovery.Collaborate with researchers to identify opportunities where generative AI can accelerate scientific progress.Work within the Machine Learning Expertise Unit and with VIB AI Studio to translate pilot projects into practical tools.Promote ethical, transparent, and responsible use of AI in line with best practices and international standards.Leverage VIB Data Core services, including high-performance computing pipelines and large-scale GPU resources, to scale ML development and deployment. Your profile PhD in machine learning, computer science, computational biology, statistics, or a related field.Knowledge of modern deep learning frameworks (e.g., PyTorch, JAX, TensorFlow).Experience developing AI applications for scientific or biomedical research, including LLM-based and agentic AI systems, is a plus.Ability to work collaboratively across disciplines and communicate effectively with researchers from different backgrounds.A strong interest in applying ML to impactful life science problems.We welcome applications from both early-career and experienced ML engineers. Our offer A stimulating, international, interdisciplinary, collaborative, and supportive work environment, which emphasizes diversity and inclusion.An environment that promotes personal development.The opportunity to pioneer applications of ML in biomedical sciences.Direct access to VIB’s Data Core services, which provide expert support in data management and high-performance computing, including optimized pipelines and large-scale GPU resources.A competitive salary and benefits package, with relocation support if needed.The chance to make a lasting impact on science, health, and society. About VIB.AI VIB.AI is the artificial intelligence initiative of VIB, designed to accelerate breakthroughs in life sciences by integrating advanced AI methods into research, discovery, and innovation. For more information, visit vib.ai/en. How to apply Please complete the application through the VIB online application tool (https://jobs.vib.be/apply/135914). Application deadline: August 31st 2026. The team develops and enhances various AI models, ML services and tools including LLM fine-tuning, alignment and optimization, RAG/Search, LLM evaluation and testing automation, feedback-based learning and guardrail for a wide range of applications in Airbnb.

You will partner with product managers, software engineers, data scientists and operation teams to brainstorm, design and develop AI products such as AI Assistant, Autonomous agent, recommendation, travel planning, and many more products that make meaningful impacts in the world of travel.
